post.title: The Post-Board Meeting Lock-In
post.body:
Last week we presented to the board and came away with a focused dose of reality. On one hand, the need for cost reduction was unavoidable due to the lack of new revenue to match our original plan. On the other hand, our focus with Canva and Jar overall was resoundingly accepted and supported.

More details below in the formal board recap!

week.text: Cost plan finalized with CFO Q1-2026
CEO Dashboard + Board recap to full team
Review new Jar roadmap with Arnoe

month.text: Revamp GTM with Canva + production lead
CHIPS Alpha launch to 20+ designers and 10 sample clients
AI for Operators course delivered to network

The 9 Levels of Leadership Every Founder Should Understand

archive.title: Week 19 — Execution Begins
archive.body:
The Chapter Three strategy is locked and we're moving into execution mode. Russ completed a successful Canva QBR in Austin — packaging and pricing landed well with Meg Moss, opening direct access to Canva's sales team. The board meeting is done, the Chapter Three update dashboard is live, and Jar is being formally handed to Arnoe this week so Russ can consolidate focus on Subscriptions and CHIPS standup. On the engineering side, Arnoe cleared the JAR AI go/no-go (decision: go) and has three external engineering resources — Neutech, Irya, and Svemir — now active on the agentic track. Consolidated Comms had its first live demo with the Ops team, scope was right-sized, and delivery is in progress.

On the revenue side, Jen's data shows churn (12K) is still outpacing new MRR (1K) — churn reduction is the single biggest lever. Rob landed all three Auto Assigner improvements (single queue, auto-run on give-back, revision logic) and is mid-negotiation on the Adobe contract. Kevin is investigating COGS creep and auditing the ETL/Tableau infrastructure, which currently has no single owner. Ops OS is rolling out to the full team this week.
